generated_comment:
A: HR is discussing period leave also now.
B: Yaar, I get being supportive, but aise toh fairness ka issue hai no? Men don't get any equivalent leave, and from company side also productivity hit hota hai — already maternity leave is a big bandwidth problem for small teams.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's biological needs and maternity protections as an unfair burden on employers and male colleagues. It encodes the 'fair/unfair' and 'productivity/cost' surface manifestations in a casual workplace discussion.
persona_text: You are Mohit. Age 27. A junior employee at a tech startup in Bengaluru. You are in the office cafeteria having lunch with three male colleagues after your female engineering manager just led a very strong all hands presentation. One colleague says she was impressive. You are responding and giving your actual opinion of her.
